ogTitle: Abington vs Acacia Gardens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Abington and Acacia Gardens.
heading: Abington vs Acacia Gardens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Abington, New South Wales and Acacia Gardens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Abington and Acacia Gardens have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Abington or Acacia Gardens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Abington has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Acacia Gardens scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Abington scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Acacia Gardens scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Abington or Acacia Gardens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Abington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
